Hi everyone, newbie question here: I am still going through ch. 6 at the moment, and since I sadly have to work this weekend, may I know if it is okay for me to skip all the story parts in ch. 7 to clear the 50 free pulls reward? I plan to retrace the story after the event is over, but I'm just concerned I will be confused if I skip the stories.
you will definitely be confused, but theres no harm in just playing first and then coming back to read it later
Did Doll die during her awakening process?
Otherwise I'd count her to the "alive" awakeners, though her version of alive is a bit less normal, given she's a brain in a vat
she died. her coworkers had to awaken her using the notes she left behind before it dissoluted completely
Must've been a scary race against time, working of notes that you can never be sure are still fully there and don't have parts of them dissolute out of your memory
oh definitely
as the person who designed the current mythag Awakening procedure she was also the first test subject
extremely baller to leave written records knowing that dissolution would literally wipe it as soon as you're dissoluted yourself
oh yeah she did all of that while also dying of dissolution
thats why her affinity story describes her as "dancing on a knifes edge"
Wonder if at that point the index for awakener potential was already made or if it was just a hail marry if she would even have awakener potential
idk how old the gnosis index system is

I'm a big fan of how important the basic characters in this game are lore wise. For being the simple version of a healer character, Doll really is shown to be one of the most important pillars of the entire mythag university
And her inferno version really shows just how much potential in her is to be a world ending threat if she was different

So the “little jasmine” she’s talking to is ho fong’s daughter presumably

As a Chinese speaker though Im happy to see words like “muma”, “baobei” preserved in the EN script. Muma is shanghaiese dialect for “mama”, and Nannan is shanghaiese for “my baby girl”
Baobei is “baby”, people who read danmei may have seen it before
she really is a shanghai night lady
Real
And Huangpu is 黄埔 a river in shanghai
Also the tune Xu hums at the end is 茉莉花, a famous chinese ancient song dating hundreds of years back

The song title means jasmine

Hua cheng
there's an older jp name which is "matsuri" or "marika" (from mo li hua)
Chinese individuals thence and now have hadden some variety of that name. So the choice to use the English can maketh sense from this purview.

embeds.title: Mo Li Hua Jasmine Flower Chinese Traditional Folk Song with English...
embeds.description:
Mo Li Hua is a Chinese Traditional Folk Song about the Jasmine Flower. Learn how to sing it with this video that includes the Chinese pin yin lyrics as well as the English translation.
